full.fieldA: economic theory (econ.TH) — bifurcation at a sustain point of a long narrow economy (twin-city emergence)
full.fieldB: gravitation (gr-qc) — scalarization of a charged black hole in Gauss-Bonnet extended gravity

verdict.decisive_evidence: Both hunters independently confirm the shared mechanism — symmetry-breaking (pitchfork) bifurcation of a nontrivial branch off a symmetric/trivial branch at a critical control parameter, with the symmetric branch going linearly unstable via a zero mode — is INDEPENDENTLY TEXTBOOK-STANDARD within EACH field. Field A: the Ikeda-Murota-Akamatsu program (two Springer monographs on 'Bifurcation Theory of Pattern Formation in Economic Geography', built on Golubitsky-Stewart group-theoretic bifurcation) treats break/sustain-point agglomeration and twin-city emergence exactly as this pitchfork; the Field-A paper itself is a routine instance. Field B: spontaneous scalarization is UNIVERSALLY defined as the scalarized branch bifurcating off the bald Schwarzschild/RN solution at a critical horizon radius/coupling, routinely cast as a Landau-type 2nd-/1st-order phase transition (arXiv 2503.11385, EPJC s10052-025-14744-3, MDPI Symmetry 12/12/2057, 2401.09846). No source states the literal A<->B identity (both hunters: 'partially_noted', literal pairing NOT FOUND after ~11-12 aggressive queries each). But the pairing is subsumed by a well-documented cross-disciplinary universal — equivariant/pitchfork symmetry-breaking bifurcation (synergetics / catastrophe theory / group-theoretic bifurcation), with a Physics-Reports-level review explicitly listing both economics and general relativity among the domains — and both fields already invoke the mechanism by the same name. A specialist in bifurcation theory would immediately recognize the equivalence. This is a genuine unpublished juxtaposition, not an original mechanism; the only physics<->economics SSB bridges that exist (Roegenian RN-thermodynamics, quantum-finance gauge-SSB) are different pairings. Novelty as discovery is LOW. Per the conservative rule, specialist_known.

h1.each_side_standard: The proposed shared mechanism — a symmetry-breaking (pitchfork) bifurcation in which a nontrivial solution branch splits off a symmetric/trivial branch at a critical value of a control parameter, rendering the symmetric branch linearly unstable via a zero mode — is INDEPENDENTLY STANDARD, indeed textbook-level, within EACH field. Field A (economic geography): the Ikeda-Murota program treats agglomeration onset (break/sustain points, twin-city and core-periphery emergence) precisely as group-theoretic symmetry-breaking pitchfork bifurcation off the uniform distribution; there are dedicated monographs and a whole 'Bifurcation Analysis in Economic Geography' literature. Field B (gravitation): spontaneous scalarization is uniformly described as the scalarized branch bifurcating off the bald Schwarzschild/Reissner-Nordstrom solution at a critical horizon radius/charge where the GR solution goes tachyonically unstable (zero mode -> nonlinear continuation), and is routinely cast as a Landau-type continuous phase transition. So neither side is novel in using this mechanism internally. What is NOT explicitly published is the LITERAL cross-field identity pairing these two specific problem classes: no paper, review, or textbook was found that states economic-geography agglomeration bifurcation and black-hole scalarization are 'the same mechanism.' The connection is only PARTIALLY NOTED, and only via the broader, generic unification that equivariant/pitchfork symmetry-breaking bifurcation is a discipline-agnostic universal mechanism (a Physics-Reports-level umbrella that lists economics and general relativity among many domains), plus a separate, mechanistically-different 'Roegenian economics' literature that maps economics onto Reissner-Nordstrom black-hole thermodynamics (not scalarization). The finder's specific A<->B bridge is therefore a genuine unpublished juxtaposition of two independently-standard instances of one abstract bifurcation, not a rediscovery of an already-stated cross-field claim.

h2.each_side_standard:
MECHANISM IS INDEPENDENTLY STANDARD IN BOTH FIELDS (strong). Field A (spatial economics): break-point/sustain-point pitchfork bifurcation of core-periphery and long-narrow-economy models producing twin cities is a mature, textbook subfield — two Springer monographs by Ikeda-Murota-Akamatsu-Takayama on 'Bifurcation Theory ... in Economic Geography', built directly on Golubitsky-Stewart group-theoretic bifurcation theory. The Field-A paper (2607.10204) is itself a routine instance. Field B (gravity): spontaneous scalarization is UNIVERSALLY DEFINED as a bifurcation off the bald (Schwarzschild/RN) branch at a critical coupling/horizon radius, and is routinely analyzed as a 2nd- or 1st-order phase transition / critical phenomenon (2503.11385, EPJC 2025 s10052-025-14744-3, MDPI Symmetry 12/12/2057, 2401.09846). So each side already frames its phenomenon in exactly this pitchfork/symmetry-breaking language on its own.

LITERAL A<->B CONNECTION: NOT FOUND after 11 aggressive queries. No review, lecture note, or cross-disciplinary paper states that twin-city/agglomeration bifurcation and black-hole scalarization are the same mechanism. The economic-geography bifurcation literature never cites gravitation/black holes; the scalarization literature never cites economics. The one apparent 'connection' surfaced by a search summarizer was a hallucinated juxtaposition of two unrelated hits. The physics<->economics bridges that DO exist are unrelated pairings (Tinbergen gravity-model of trade; 'market as self-gravitating body' for crypto; SSB of stock-market valuation / quantum finance) — none link economic geography to scalarization.

WHY 'partially_noted' NOT 'not_found': the proposed shared mechanism is nothing more specific than the generic symmetry-breaking pitchfork bifurcation of a trivial/symmetric branch at a critical control parameter. That template is a well-documented cross-disciplinary universal (synergetics/catastrophe theory/group-theoretic bifurcation) that demonstrably subsumes BOTH application classes, and both fields already invoke it by the same name. The specific pairing is unpublished, but it is 'covered by a broader unification' rather than a genuinely new bridge — so its novelty as a discovery is LOW. Verdict: adjacent/generic, not an original cross-field identity.

full.fieldA: nonlinear/pattern physics (nlin.PS) — Thouless pumping of gap solitons mediated by finite Bogoliubov excitations
full.fieldB: particle physics (hep-ph) — right-handed neutrino production by an axion-like inflaton (leptogenesis)

verdict.id: S7: 2606.31006 (nlin.PS Thouless pumping of gap solitons via finite Bogoliubov excitations) <-> 2607.13592 (hep-ph right-handed neutrino production by axion-like inflaton, leptogenesis)
verdict.decisive_evidence: Both hunters independently confirm the shared mechanism — non-adiabatic Bogoliubov mode occupation in the instantaneous Hamiltonian-diagonal basis — is textbook-standard within EACH field: the nlin.PS paper (2606.31006) natively frames its dynamics as 'finite Bogoliubov excitations under near-adiabatic ramping,' and the hep-ph paper (2607.13592) itself reconciles 'the fermion basis used to identify non-adiabatic production' with 'the Hamiltonian-diagonal basis used to define instantaneous occupation numbers.' This is the Parker (1969) Bogoliubov-coefficient particle-production formalism, foundational in both domains. Moreover, the cross-field equivalence is not merely recognizable but already explicitly published at the mechanism-class level: the analog-gravity / analog-cosmological-reheating program uses the IDENTICAL non-adiabatic Bogoliubov mechanism to equate condensed-matter BEC quasiparticle production with inflaton-decay/reheating particle production (arXiv 2008.02290 'Analog cosmological reheating in an ultracold Bose gas' = PRA 104.023302; review arXiv 1208.4729; Nat. Commun. 2022 quantum-fluid-of-light). What is NOT in print is a single source naming the two exact endpoints (Thouless-pumped gap solitons AND axion-inflaton right-handed-neutrino leptogenesis) as one mechanism — which is why this is not known_crossfield for this precise identity. But a specialist in either field would instantly recognize the equivalence, and an established published unification already covers the mechanism class, so this is comfortably specialist_known, not novel_looking.

h1.each_side_standard: The mechanism is independently STANDARD and textbook within EACH field. Field A (nlin.PS / BEC): 'Bogoliubov excitations from non-adiabatic ramping' is the native framing of the gap-soliton pumping paper itself, and instantaneous-Hamiltonian-diagonalized Bogoliubov mode occupation is textbook BEC physics (Bogoliubov-de Gennes). Field B (hep-ph leptogenesis): the paper explicitly frames production as 'non-adiabatic' and reconciles the 'fermion basis used to identify non-adiabatic production' with the 'Hamiltonian-diagonal basis used to define instantaneous occupation numbers' — Bogoliubov-coefficient particle production in a time-dependent (inflaton/gravitational) background has been the standard cosmological formalism since Parker (1969) and the 1981 thesis on the topic. So the proposed 'shared mechanism' is not novel in either field; it is each field's own default language. VERDICT = partially_noted (not explicitly_published): NO source names Thouless-pumped gap solitons AND axion-inflaton right-handed-neutrino leptogenesis together as one mechanism. However, the specific pairing is subsumed by a broadly established, explicitly-published unification — the analog-gravity / analog-cosmological-reheating program (arXiv 2008.02290 = PRA 104.023302; review arXiv 1208.4729; Nat. Commun. 2022 quantum-fluid-of-light) — which uses the IDENTICAL non-adiabatic Bogoliubov mechanism to equate condensed-matter BEC/soliton excitations with inflaton-decay/reheating particle production (the same preheating physics the leptogenesis paper relies on). The A<->B bridge is therefore a specific instance of a mechanism already published as cross-field-universal, just not with these two exact endpoints connected in print.

h2.each_side_standard: Yes — the mechanism is independently standard within BOTH fields, and neither the connection nor its inverse is novel inside its own domain. FIELD A (nlin.PS / BEC / condensed matter): 'finite Bogoliubov excitations' driving adiabatic-theorem breakdown in Thouless pumping of gap solitons is a canonical, heavily-reused device — the source paper (2606.31006) states it directly, and it recurs across the soliton-pumping cluster (2404.13823, 2401.09148, 2601.01355); the underlying non-adiabatic Landau-Zener-Stuckelberg transition is the standard account of topological-pump breakdown (2408.01629, Nature Phys s41567-023-02145-w). FIELD B (hep-ph / cosmology): Bogoliubov-coefficient particle production with non-adiabatic transitions in preheating/inflation is foundational and reviewed (2112.02444), formalized via exact-WKB / Landau-Zener / Stokes phenomena (2104.02312, 2203.04497); the target neutrino paper (2607.13592) itself computes fermion abundance with the Bogoliubov approach and 'repeated non-adiabatic production events.' The GENERAL bridge between the two fields — condensed-matter/BEC Bogoliubov quasiparticle production as an analog of cosmological particle creation via the shared adiabatic-breakdown skeleton — is extensively published (the analog-gravity / analogue-cosmology program: Nature Comms s41467-022-30603-1, arXiv 1203.1173, MPLA quasiparticle-universes), and the exact same LZS mechanism demonstrably appears on both sides. What is NOT published anywhere I could find is the SPECIFIC A<->B identity as stated: nobody has written that Thouless pumping of gap solitons is 'the same mechanism' as right-handed-neutrino/leptogenesis production by an axion-like inflaton. The existing bridges are always scalar/phonon quasiparticles <-> scalar cosmological creation (Hawking/de Sitter), never topological soliton pumping <-> fermionic leptogenesis. Hence partially_noted: the identity is subsumed by a broad, well-established unification (Bogoliubov transformation + adiabatic breakdown; analog gravity) and is standard on each side, but the particular field-pair pairing is not literally asserted in the literature.

full.fieldA: cosmology (astro-ph.CO) — Stage-IV combined-probe forecast for dark-matter gravitons / string theory
full.fieldB: signal processing (eess.SP) — parametric diffraction-based object sensing, estimation limits

verdict.decisive_evidence: The "shared mechanism" is the Fisher information matrix / Cramér-Rao bound (Rao 1945, Cramér 1946) — the single most standard estimation-theory theorem, and the SAME named object in both fields, not an analogy. Cosmological Fisher forecasting was historically imported DIRECTLY from signal-processing/estimation theory (Tegmark, Taylor & Heavens 1997), and cosmology reviews (arXiv:1205.3984, 1404.2854) derive it explicitly from the Cramér-Rao lower bound. In eess.SP the CRB is the textbook fundamental estimation-limit for diffraction/radar/sensing (Kay, Van Trees). The cross-field universality is explicitly published, not merely specialist-inferable: cross-disciplinary reviews frame Fisher/CRB as one domain-agnostic bound spanning statistics-physics-signal processing, and Zmuidzinas 2003 (JOSAA) is a direct astronomy<->signal-processing CRB bridge. Zero novelty as a cross-field insight.

closest_hits.title: Statistical Signal Processing in Engineering — Cramér–Rao Bound chapter (Wiley/IEEE)
closest_hits.relevance: Textbook chapter presenting CRB as THE fundamental estimation benchmark in signal processing — establishes the mechanism as canonical/standard within Field B independent of any cosmology link.

closest_hits.title: Fisher information and the Cramér-Rao bound across disciplines (search synthesis; e.g. Stanford STATS200 notes, cross-field usage)
closest_hits.relevance: General statistics treatment framing Fisher/CRB as the universal minimum-variance bound applied across geophysics, biology, reconstruction, and signal processing — the broader unification that subsumes the proposed A↔B connection but does not name cosmology forecasting and diffraction sensing together specifically.

h1.each_side_standard: Both sides are independently textbook-standard, which is the decisive point: the 'shared mechanism' is not a novel hidden bridge but a domain-general statistics theorem (Fisher information matrix / Cramér-Rao bound; Rao 1945, Cramér 1946). FIELD A (cosmology, astro-ph.CO): Fisher forecasting is THE canonical survey-forecasting tool — Tegmark, Taylor & Heavens (1997) introduced it by explicitly borrowing from statistical estimation theory, and reviews such as 'On the Validity of Cosmological Fisher Matrix Forecasts' (arXiv:1205.3984) and 'Generalised Fisher Matrices' (arXiv:1404.2854) derive it straight from the Cramér-Rao lower bound. Completely standard. FIELD B (signal processing, eess.SP): the Cramér-Rao bound is THE fundamental parametric estimation-limit benchmark, covered in every statistical-signal-processing textbook (Kay; 'Statistical Signal Processing in Engineering') and used universally in radar/ISAC/array/diffraction sensing (e.g. arXiv:2209.13182, arXiv:2406.01937). Completely standard. CROSS-FIELD IDENTITY: Because the mechanism is the SAME universal statistical object in both fields, the connection is real but trivial/expected rather than a discovered analogy. Sources repeatedly describe Fisher/CRB as a cross-disciplinary universal bound (statistics → signal processing, geophysics, biology, quantum metrology, gravitational waves, cosmology, radar), and the cosmology→estimation-theory transfer is historically documented (Tegmark et al. imported it from SP/estimation theory). However, no single source is dedicated to literally asserting THIS specific 'cosmological Fisher forecast ↔ signal-processing diffraction-sensing CRB' equivalence — none is needed, since both are recognized as instances of one textbook theorem. Hence 'partially_noted': the A↔B link is fully covered by the broader, explicitly-published unification (the Cramér-Rao bound as the universal estimation-precision limit) even though no paper is devoted to naming these two particular problem types side by side. The proposed 'bridge' therefore carries essentially zero novelty as a cross-field insight.

closest_hits.title: Generalisations of Fisher Matrices (Heavens et al., Entropy / arXiv 1606.06455)
closest_hits.relevance: Cosmology Fisher-forecast methodology paper framed around the Cramer-Rao inequality, treating the cosmological Fisher matrix as an instance of the general statistical estimation bound.

h2.each_side_standard: Independently textbook-standard in BOTH fields — this is a shared, domain-agnostic statistical tool, not a genuine hidden bridge. Field A (cosmology): the Fisher information matrix / Cramer-Rao bound is the universal forecasting method (Tegmark 1997; Tegmark, Taylor & Heavens 1997; Coe quick-start guide; Wittman 'Fisher for Beginners'; Euclid forecast-validation; ubiquitous in every Stage-IV combined-probe forecast). Field B (signal processing / eess.SP): the Cramer-Rao bound is the bedrock of estimation theory (Kay, Van Trees) and the standard fundamental-limit tool for radar and diffraction-based/optical object sensing and super-resolution. Both fields explicitly present their method as an application of the SAME Cramer-Rao/Fisher bound — cosmological Fisher forecasting was in fact imported into astronomy directly FROM statistical estimation theory. Status set to 'partially_noted' rather than 'explicitly_published' only because no single source literally pairs 'Stage-IV cosmology combined-probe forecast' with 'diffraction object-sensing CRB' as a named analogy; but the identity is fully covered by the well-known broader unification (Fisher/CRB is one statistical tool), and cross-disciplinary sources (Zmuidzinas 2003; the diffraction-imaging CRB literature; the 'applications to physics' review) already bridge astronomical sensing and signal-processing estimation via this exact bound. The 'connection' is a trivial common-statistical-tool coincidence, not a novel cross-field discovery.

full.fieldA: statistics (stat.ME) — higher-order spillover effects under partial interference (Horvitz-Thompson/Hajek)
full.fieldB: machine learning (cs.LG) — kernel-weighted importance sampling for off-policy evaluation in contextual bandits

verdict.decisive_evidence: The importance-sampling/IPW identity (Horvitz-Thompson = unnormalized IS, Hajek = self-normalized IS, correcting a behavior->target distribution shift) is textbook-standard within BOTH fields independently AND explicitly published as a cross-field identity. Field B explicitly imports Hajek: Swaminathan & Joachims (2015) name SNIPS 'the Hajek estimator' and every OPE paper cites Horvitz & Thompson 1952 for IPS. Field A treats HT/Hajek IPW as canonical for spillover under partial interference (Biometrika 2016; arXiv 2312.01234). The general unification is explicitly published (Bottou et al. 2013; arXiv 2204.14121 'Inverse Probability Weighting: from Survey Sampling to Evidence Estimation' ties HT=unnormalized IS and Hajek=self-normalized IS across survey sampling, causal inference, and Monte-Carlo IS). Most decisively, IntOPE (arXiv 2408.13484) co-locates the exact field pair — off-policy evaluation in contextual bandits under network interference/spillover using Horvitz-Thompson/IPW/SNIPW. Hunter 2's 'partially_noted' downgrade rests only on the absence of a single source stating the ultra-narrow sub-area pairing, but the proposed shared mechanism as worded is the general IS/IPW identity, which is ubiquitously published.

closest_hits.title: On inverse probability-weighted estimators in the presence of interference (Biometrika 2016)
closest_hits.relevance: Field-A standardness: Horvitz-Thompson and Hajek-type IPW estimators are the canonical tools for direct/indirect/spillover effects under (partial/general) interference; foundational for the exact Field-A problem.

closest_hits.title: Individualized Policy Evaluation and Learning under Clustered Network Interference
closest_hits.relevance: Bridges policy evaluation/learning with partial (clustered network) interference via an additive IPW estimator — same mechanism, spanning the OPE and interference literatures.

h1.each_side_standard: The mechanism is textbook-standard within BOTH fields independently. Field A (stat.ME, spillover under partial interference): the Horvitz-Thompson estimator and its self-normalized Hajek variant are THE canonical IPW estimators for direct/indirect/spillover effects — Hudgens & Halloran (2008), Tchetgen Tchetgen & VanderWeele, Aronow & Samii, and 'On inverse probability-weighted estimators in the presence of interference' (Biometrika 2016). Field B (cs.LG, off-policy evaluation in contextual bandits): IPS/IPW is universally identified with importance sampling and the Horvitz-Thompson estimator as the standard baseline, and self-normalized IPS (SNIPS) is explicitly named 'the Hajek estimator' by Swaminathan & Joachims (2015). So Horvitz-Thompson=unnormalized IS and Hajek=self-normalized IS is a commonplace within each field, not a discovery.\n\nThe cross-field identity itself is ALSO explicitly published, not merely implied. Off-policy evaluation is widely stated to be equivalent to causal inference of stochastic interventions (importance sampling correcting behavior->target distribution shift), and Bottou et al. (2013) explicitly unified importance-sampling causal inference/counterfactual reasoning with policy evaluation. Most tellingly, IntOPE (arXiv 2408.13484) sits at the exact intersection of the two proposed subfields: it performs off-policy evaluation in contextual bandits UNDER interference/spillover using Horvitz-Thompson/IPW/SNIPW estimators. Verdict: the proposed A<->B connection is well-trodden prior art; the mechanism is a known common denominator, and dedicated work already spans both problem types simultaneously.

But it frames its 'IntIPW' as an ADAPTATION of IPW to interference, not as an explicit statement that the two literatures share one identical mechanism — so it is adjacent, not a literal A<->B identity claim.

(PDF text was not cleanly machine-readable in fetch, but the framing is well established across the field.)

closest_hits.title: The Self-Normalized Estimator for Counterfactual Learning (Swaminathan & Joachims 2015)
closest_hits.relevance: Establishes self-normalized IPS (SNIPS) — explicitly the Hajek estimator — as the standard OPE/counterfactual-learning tool in the ML (Field B) literature; confirms Hajek = self-normalized IS is textbook within contextual-bandit OPE.

closest_hits.title: On the admissibility of Horvitz-Thompson estimator for estimating causal effects under network interference (arXiv 2312.01234)
closest_hits.relevance: Confirms Horvitz-Thompson / Hajek with exposure/inclusion probabilities are THE canonical design-based estimators for spillover/interference in stat.ME (Field A); demonstrates the mechanism is fully standard within Field A independently.

closest_hits.title: Counterfactual Reasoning and Learning Systems (Bottou et al. 2013, arXiv 1209.2355)
closest_hits.relevance: Foundational work casting off-policy/counterfactual policy evaluation as importance sampling and connecting it to causal inference / IPW; part of the broad, long-standing bridge between ML policy evaluation and the causal-inference IPW family.

h2.each_side_standard: The importance-sampling / inverse-probability-weighting mechanism is unambiguously STANDARD and textbook within EACH field independently. Field A (stat.ME, interference/spillover): Horvitz-Thompson (unnormalized IS) and Hajek (self-normalized/weighted IS) estimators built on exposure/inclusion probabilities are the canonical design-based tools for spillover effects under partial interference — the target paper itself uses them, and multiple works (e.g. arXiv 2312.01234 on HT admissibility under network interference, PMC clustered-network-interference) treat this as routine. Field B (cs.LG, contextual-bandit OPE): the IPS estimator IS the Horvitz-Thompson estimator (every OPE paper cites Horvitz & Thompson 1952 as its origin) and self-normalized IPS/SNIPS IS the Hajek estimator (Swaminathan & Joachims 2015). So the mechanism is not novel in either field. On the CROSS-FIELD identity: the GENERAL bridge 'off-policy-evaluation importance sampling = causal-inference IPW = survey-sampling Horvitz-Thompson/Hajek' is explicitly and ubiquitously published (Bottou et al. 2013; Li 2019; the 2204.14121 IPW-unification, which however excludes bandits and interference). But NO single source I found literally states the NARROW pairing at issue — 'higher-order spillover estimation under partial interference' <-> 'kernel-weighted IS for OPE in contextual bandits' — as one and the same mechanism. The closest, IntOPE (arXiv 2408.13484), brings both fields into one paper (IPW-based OPE adapted to network interference, citing both Horvitz-Thompson 1952 and the spillover literature) but frames it as an adaptation rather than an explicit equivalence claim. Hence: the mechanism-level connection is covered by a broader, well-known unification and is adjacent/hinted for this exact field pair, but not literally asserted for these two specific sub-areas — 'partially_noted', not 'explicitly_published'.

full.fieldA: neuroscience/ML (q-bio.NC) — emergent generalization by representation learning in ANNs
full.fieldB: economic theory (econ.TH) — choice at finite capacity, bounded agent as an information channel, Walrasian demand

verdict.decisive_evidence: Independent third-party sources explicitly make the cross-field mechanism identity, not just each side separately. Ortega & Braun 2015 (arXiv 1512.06789) present ONE rate-distortion/free-energy functional with a single capacity (inverse-temperature) parameter interpolating perfectly-rational (full capacity) to prior/habit (low capacity), explicitly named as spanning "AI, reinforcement learning, computational neuroscience AND economics." Leibfried & Braun 2016 (UAI, arXiv 1602.08332) apply the economic finite-capacity-channel formalism directly to neural-network representation learning and generalization/overfitting — the Field-A half. Plus rational-inattention-for-DNN-classification (2102.04594) and rate-distortion generalization across humans+deep models (2603.01568). Each side is separately textbook: economics = rational inattention as the rate-distortion problem with Walrasian demand as the infinite-capacity limit (Sims 2003; JEL 2023 review); ML = information bottleneck as rate-distortion representation learning where compression drives generalization (Tishby & Zaslavsky; eLife 2020). The only element unique to the target paper 2607.12412 is the "Walrasian demand recovery" relabeling of the standard infinite-capacity limit, and that paper is one endpoint (not independent prior art) and itself frames its contribution as a unification of already-connected literatures. Both hunters independently converged on the same subsuming prior art. Not a hidden discovery.

closest_hits.title: Choice at Finite Capacity: The Bounded Agent as an Information Channel and the Recovery of Walrasian Demand (arXiv 2607.12412 — the Field B paper ITSELF)
closest_hits.relevance: NOT independent prior art (it is one endpoint), but decisive: it LITERALLY states the A<->B identity — 'a machine running a capacity-limited policy, an information-bottleneck representation or a rate-distortion reinforcement learner, is to the theory the same object as the human consumer'; 'the economic decision-maker as a finite channel, the deep network compressing its input... are one object at different budgets'; cites Sims rational inattention; Walrasian demand = beta->infinity (infinite-capacity) limit. So the finder's 'discovery' is self-stated by one of its own two papers.

closest_hits.title: Information-Theoretic Bounded Rationality (Ortega & Braun, arXiv 1512.06789)
closest_hits.relevance: Independent BROADER unification: one free-energy / rate-distortion functional with an inverse-temperature (capacity) parameter interpolating perfectly-rational (full capacity) <-> prior/default (low capacity, habit), explicitly named as spanning 'artificial intelligence, reinforcement learning, computational neuroscience and economics.' This is the A<->B mechanism-level bridge published years earlier, minus the specific 'Walrasian demand / representation-learning-generalization' framings.

closest_hits.title: Bounded Rational Decision-Making in Feedforward Neural Networks (Leibfried & Braun, UAI 2016, arXiv 1602.08332)
closest_hits.relevance: Direct econ->ANN bridge: models each neuron / the whole net as a bounded-rational decision-maker = 'information-theoretic channel with limited transmission rate'; the capacity constraint becomes information-theoretic regularization that improves generalization / prevents overfitting on MNIST. Explicitly connects the finite-capacity-channel (economics) framing to neural-network representation learning and generalization — the Field A side.

closest_hits.title: Rational inattention in neural coding for economic choice (bioRxiv 2024, 10.1101/2024.09.20.614193)
closest_hits.relevance: Bridges the economics side (rational inattention as finite-capacity channel) with neural coding of economic choice — the same rate-distortion/capacity mechanism spanning econ choice and (biological) neural representations.

closest_hits.title: Systems of Bounded Rational Agents with Information-Theoretic Constraints (Genewein/Hihn/Braun, Neural Computation 2019)
closest_hits.relevance: Extends the info-theoretic bounded-rationality / rate-distortion capacity principle to networks of agents and abstraction/hierarchy — same single-capacity-parameter machinery presented as domain-general (decision-making + networks).

closest_hits.title: Rate-distortion theory of neural coding and its implications for working memory (Bates & Jacobs, eLife 2020, article 79450)
closest_hits.relevance: Establishes that rate-distortion / capacity-limited compression is the STANDARD normative account of neural representation on the ML/neuroscience side (Field A) — capacity parameter trades fidelity for compression, yielding structured 'distortions' (prototypization/specialization).

closest_hits.title: Rational Inattention: A Review (Maćkowiak, Matějka & Wiederholt, JEL 2023)
closest_hits.relevance: Establishes that the finite-Shannon-capacity / mutual-information-cost channel model of the consumer is TEXTBOOK-STANDARD in economics (Field B), with the fully-rational (Walrasian) agent as the unlimited-capacity limit — so Field B's mechanism is not novel.

closest_hits.title: Deep Learning and the Information Bottleneck Principle (Tishby & Zaslavsky, arXiv 1503.02406)
closest_hits.relevance: Establishes the information-bottleneck / rate-distortion view of representation learning and generalization as STANDARD on the ANN side (Field A): a capacity/compression trade-off where compressing the input drives generalization.

h1.each_side_standard: The mechanism is textbook-standard WITHIN EACH field independently, so it is not novel on either side. Field B (economics): the 'bounded agent as a finite-Shannon-capacity information channel choosing under a mutual-information cost' IS rational inattention (Sims 2003/2010); it is a mainstream research program (JEL 2023 review) and it is a KNOWN standard result that the classical Walrasian/utility-maximizing consumer is recovered exactly as the unlimited-capacity (zero information-cost) limit — the Field B paper's headline result restates this canon. Field A (ML / neuroscience): the information-bottleneck / rate-distortion view of representation learning, where a single capacity/compression parameter (beta) trades fidelity against compression and where compression itself drives generalization, is standard (Tishby-Zaslavsky IB; rate-distortion neural coding, eLife 2020). CROSS-FIELD: the connection is NOT independently novel either. It is covered by a broader, well-established unification — Ortega & Braun's 'information-theoretic bounded rationality' (arXiv 1512.06789; Neural Computation 2019) explicitly presents one free-energy/rate-distortion functional with a capacity/inverse-temperature parameter interpolating perfectly-rational -> prior/habit as spanning 'AI, reinforcement learning, computational neuroscience and economics,' and the UAI-2016 paper (1602.08332) concretely applies the economic finite-capacity-channel formalism to neural-network representation learning and generalization. Decisively, the Field B paper (2607.12412) ITSELF states the identity verbatim ('a machine... information-bottleneck representation or a rate-distortion reinforcement learner... is to the theory the same object as the human consumer'; 'the economic decision-maker as a finite channel, the deep network compressing its input... are one object at different budgets'), so the cross-field bridge is self-published by one of the two endpoints rather than being a hidden discovery. Verdict = partially_noted (not explicitly_published) ONLY because I did not find an INDEPENDENT third source whose thesis is the exact pairing 'emergent generalization by representation learning in ANNs <-> recovery of Walrasian demand'; but the pairing is squarely subsumed by the published information-theoretic-bounded-rationality / rational-inattention = rate-distortion = information-bottleneck unification, and both halves are standard. This bridge should be treated as adjacent/known, not as a genuine hidden cross-field discovery.

It is the umbrella that subsumes both fields, but never names ANN representation-learning generalization or Walrasian demand recovery specifically.

closest_hits.title: Rationally Inattentive Utility Maximization for Interpretable Deep Image Classification (arXiv 2102.04594)
closest_hits.relevance: Genuine pre-existing econ<->ML bridge: imports the economics rational-inattention framework directly onto deep neural-network classifiers, demonstrating the two domains share the same information-cost machinery. Adjacent to, not identical with, the proposed generalization<->Walrasian-demand pairing.

closest_hits.title: Rate-Distortion Signatures of Generalization and Information Trade-off in Humans and Deep Vision Models (arXiv 2603.01568)
closest_hits.relevance: Ties GENERALIZATION to rate-distortion jointly across humans and deep vision models (the ML/neuro half of the claim), but stops at perception/vision and does not cross into economic consumer demand.

closest_hits.title: Information theory in economics, Part I: Rational Inattention (The Information Structuralist)
closest_hits.relevance: Confirms rational inattention IS the distortion-rate/rate-distortion problem in economics (the econ half is standard), but WebFetch verified it makes NO mention of machine learning, representation learning, or the information bottleneck.

closest_hits.title: On Rate-Distortion Theory in Capacity-Limited Cognition & Reinforcement Learning (NeurIPS 2022 InfoCog workshop, Arumugam et al.)
closest_hits.relevance: Frames capacity-limited cognition and RL as rate-distortion (the mechanism the paper cites as prior art), unifying cognition/ML but not reaching Walrasian demand theory.

closest_hits.title: An Information Bottleneck Asset Pricing Model (arXiv 2507.23218)
closest_hits.relevance: Applies the ML information-bottleneck to an economics/finance problem — another adjacent IB<->economics crossing, but asset pricing rather than consumer/Walrasian demand and generalization.

closest_hits.title: Choice at Finite Capacity: The Bounded Agent as an Information Channel and the Recovery of Walrasian Demand (arXiv 2607.12412)
closest_hits.relevance: The Field-B target paper itself. It is the ONLY source that literally states the A<->B identity ('the consumer of demand theory ... and the capacity-limited learner of machine learning are one object at different budgets'), and it explicitly frames this as a unification of existing literatures (citing Tishby, Arumugam & Roy, Lai & Gershman, Alemi), claiming novelty only for the demand-theoretic consequence — so it is not independent prior art for the connection.

h2.each_side_standard: The underlying mechanism is textbook-standard within EACH field independently. ML/neuroscience side: the information bottleneck is explicitly a rate-distortion problem (Tishby et al. 1999; Tishby & Zaslavsky 2015), and capacity-limited/compressed representation learning as the driver of generalization is a well-established view (Alemi et al.; Arumugam & Roy 2021; Lai & Gershman 2021). Economics side: rational inattention is mathematically the rate-distortion / distortion-rate problem (Sims 2003; Matejka-McKay), a standard equivalence, and 'infinite-capacity limit = classical/Walrasian demand, zero-capacity = habit' is the specific contribution of the target paper. So the shared object (a finite-capacity rate-distortion channel with one capacity knob) is a recognized bridge and each side is independently standard. What is NOT independently published is the exact A<->B pairing 'emergent ANN generalization by representation learning == Walrasian demand recovery of the bounded consumer.' The broad unification of economic choice and ML capacity-limited learning under one information-cost framework IS published (Ortega & Braun 2015; rational-inattention-for-DNN-classification 2102.04594; rate-distortion-generalization-in-humans-and-deep-models 2603.01568), which is why this is 'partially_noted' rather than 'not_found' — but the only source stating this precise generalization<->Walrasian identity is the target paper 2607.12412 itself, which presents it as a unification of already-connected literatures, not as an independently pre-existing cross-field result. No third-party source was found asserting THIS specific identity, so it is not 'explicitly_published.'
